assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 6 lists exactly the 6 scoped files. Files to Modify: (1) advisory/service/advisory.rs, (2) advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, (3) advisory/model/mod.rs. Files to Create: (1) advisory/model/severity_summary.rs, (2) advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs, (3) tests/api/advisory_summary.rs. Step 9 mentions docs/api.md 'may need updating' but this is a speculative verification check, not a committed implementation change.

assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 10 shows the commit message: 'feat(advisory): add severity aggregation endpoint for SBOM advisories' (Conventional Commits format with type 'feat', scope 'advisory', colon, description). The footer contains 'Implements TC-9201' referencing the Jira ticket.

assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 10, line 202: 'With trailer: `--trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code'`'

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md line 8: 'Branch Name: TC-9201'. Step 5: 'git checkout main / git pull / git checkout -b TC-9201' and 'Branch TC-9201 created from target branch main'. Step 10: 'gh pr create --base main' referencing the target branch.

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md 'Service Conventions' section: 'Return type: All methods return Result<T, AppError> where T is the domain type' and 'Error handling: Errors are wrapped using .context("descriptive message") (anyhow-style), producing AppError variants'. 'Module Structure Conventions' section: 'Domain module layout: Every domain module follows the model/ + service/ + endpoints/ tripartite structure'. Also 'Error Handling Conventions' section: 'Error type: AppError enum implements IntoResponse for Axum' and 'Context wrapping: All fallible operations use .context("message") to add context before converting to AppError'.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 1 table: 'Target Branch | main'. Step 1 subsection 'Target Branch Extraction': 'Target Branch is **main**. This will be used as the base for branch creation (Step 5) and as the --base argument for gh pr create (Step 10).' Step 5: 'git checkout main / git pull / git checkout -b TC-9201 / Branch TC-9201 created from target branch main'. Step 10: 'gh pr create --base main'.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 1.5 'Verify Description Integrity': 'Would retrieve issue comments via jira.get_issue_comments(TC-9201) and search for comments starting with [sdlc-workflow] Description digest:.' 'Result: No description digest comment found. Logging warning and proceeding normally: "No description digest found -- skipping integrity check. This task may have been created before digest tracking was introduced."' 'This is backward-compatible behavior -- execution is not blocked when no digest exists.'
